meta.title: Behaviour-Based Safety Program Design — {site / crew / operation}
meta.subtitle: Non-punitive BBS program · ABC analysis · observable behaviours · ISO 45001 clause 5.4 worker participation
meta.author: {program owner role}
meta.deid_notice: This BBS program is non-punitive by design: all observation data is role-labelled or anonymous and used for trending and learning, never individual discipline. No card records a nameable individual; no team breakdown of fewer than 5 is published (secondary suppression applied); metrics are trended by behaviour category, never by person, and no re-identification key appears. Decision-support; a competent person must review this program before roll-out.

sections.text: A non-punitive behaviour-based-safety observation program for the {site / crew / operation}, designed around observable, site-specific behaviours and ABC analysis. At-risk behaviours are trended to systemic causes and routed to hierarchy-ranked system fixes — never to 'retrain the worker' or discipline. Grounded in ISO 45001 clause 5.4 (worker participation).

Cards are role-labelled or anonymous, voluntary, and used for trending and learning — never individual sanction. Participation is voluntary; the program observes the work and the behaviour, not the worker.

For each target behaviour the antecedent (the real trigger), the observable behaviour, and the consequence are mapped; the system is designed so the SAFE behaviour carries the soon / certain / positive consequence.

sections.caption: Each behaviour is observable and site-specific; a non-observable slogan is refused, not listed.

items: Each item is observable and site-specific, tied to a named task/area (never 'work safely' / 'be careful').
Role-labelled or anonymous by default — no nameable individual is recorded.
Voluntary participation; two-way, immediate observer feedback (positive AND at-risk discussed on the spot).
Data is used for trending and learning, never individual discipline.

metrics.label: Percent-safe
metrics.caption: trended by behaviour category, never by person

sections.text: Metrics are defined per KB-DATA-BBS-METRICS and trended by behaviour category, never by person. The <5 small-cell suppression applies to any team breakdown — a percent-safe for a 4-person crew is suppressed, with secondary suppression so it cannot be back-calculated.
At-risk behaviours trended to systemic causes

rows.title: {at-risk behaviour category}
rows.description: {the recurring systemic driver this category points to}
rows.evidence: {aggregated card trend — category-level, no individual named}

sections.text: System fixes (hierarchy of controls — never retrain-the-worker)

rows.control: {eliminate / substitute / engineer / administrate fix for the at-risk category}

sections.text: Each trended at-risk category is routed through the controls engine to a hierarchy-ranked system fix. 'Retrain the worker', 'be more careful', or discipline as the sole response is rejected as a hierarchy-of-controls defect.

rows.action: {system-fix action for a trended at-risk category}

sections.text: observe → discuss (two-way, positive + at-risk) → record card (de-identified) → aggregate / trend by behaviour category → feed systemic fixes via controls + owned/dated actions → close the loop back to observers and the workforce. Metrics reviewed {cadence}; cards reviewed {cadence}.
